data.title: FXR Pro Fish Named Title Sponsor For 2025 Bassmaster Elite Season Opener
data.description:
FXR Pro Fish Named Title Sponsor For 2025 Bassmaster Elite Season Opener

The 2025 FXR Pro Fish Team Bassmaster Elite Will Hit The Water At St. Johns River

FXR Pro Fish, a brand driven by bringing innovation to the water, is proud to announce its partnership with the Bassmaster Elite series as the title sponsor for the first round of the new season. The 2025 FXR Pro Fish Bassmaster Elite kicks off at St. Johns River in Palatka, FL on February 20-23, 2025. The event will also be broadcast live on Fox Sports 1 and Fox Sports App, as well as the Bassmaster livestream on Bassmaster.com and YouTubeTV.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250218621439/en/

Trey McKinney FXR Pro Fish Angler (Photo: Business Wire)

"Our partnership with the Bassmaster organization as the title sponsor for the first round of the series exemplifies our dedication to the sport,” said Brian Danielson, Chief Executive Officer of FXR Pro Fish and FXR Racing. “It also comes at a perfect time for the FXR Pro Fish brand, as it follows the launch of our Spring 2025 lineup. We are excited to see our Elite roster take to the water in their fresh gear.”

The full roster of FXR Pro Fish Elite anglers includes Trey McKinney, Cooper Galant, Justin Hammer, Bryan New, Logan Parks, Matt Roberston, Bryan Schmitt, JT Thompkins, Tyler Williams, and rookie Beau Browning. The FXR Pro Fish Elite anglers will set out on the water to take down the 93-pound-6-ounces record of 2024 and kick the season off with a victory.

“Our lineup of anglers is incredibly stacked, so we are excited to watch these guys hit the water,” said Chris Meyeraan, Brand Director of FXR Pro Fish. “We may be a newer brand to the fishing world, but we are here to make our mark on the sport.”

The FXR Pro Fish Anglers will be back in action at round two of the Bassmaster Elite series at Lake Okeechobee in Okeechobee, FL from February 27 - March 2, 2025. To learn more about the FXR Pro Fish apparel or the Bassmaster series, please visit: https://fxrracing.com/pages/pro-fish or https://www.bassmaster.com/.

MEDIA CONTACT:
Neil Storz
(805)794-5352
neil@storzconsult.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250218621439/en/

data.title: DOGE Fires FDA Employees Reviewing Musk's Neuralink, Reuters Reports, Citing Sources
data.description:
--Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency over the weekend fired U.S. Food and Drug Administration employees reviewing Musk's brain-implant company Neuralink as part of a broader plan to cut the size of the federal workforce, Reuters reports, citing two people familiar with the matter.

--About 20 people in the FDA's office of neurological and physical medicine devices were affected by the cuts, Reuters reports, citing sources. The loss of these employees will hamper the FDA's ability to quickly and safely process medical device applications of all sorts, including Neuralink's, Reuters reports, citing sources and outside experts. 




  Full article at https://www.reuters.com/world/us/fda-staff-reviewing-musks-neuralink-were-included-doge-employee-firings-sources-2025-02-17/ 





  Write to (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix) 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 18, 2025 12:11 ET (17:11 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: SUAVE BRANDS COMPANY UNLEASHES ITS BIGGEST PRODUCT DROP IN DECADES — 34 NEW INNOVATIONS ACROSS HAIR, BODY & MEN'S CARE
data.description:
SUAVE BRANDS COMPANY UNLEASHES ITS BIGGEST PRODUCT DROP IN DECADES -- 34 NEW INNOVATIONS ACROSS HAIR, BODY & MEN'S CARE

SUAVE BRANDS COMPANY UNLEASHES ITS BIGGEST PRODUCT DROP IN DECADES -- 34 NEW INNOVATIONS ACROSS HAIR, BODY & MEN'S CARE

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Feb. 18, 2025

Newly independent Suave doubles down on innovation, bringing salon-worthy formulas, trending ingredients, and indulgent scents—all at unbeatable prices

NEW YORK, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Suave Brands Company is shaking up the beauty aisle with 34 new products, marking its boldest innovation push in years. Known for delivering quality at an accessible price, Suave is bringing luxury-level performance to everyday essentials—without the hefty price tag.

 Suave Brands is shaking up the beauty aisle with 34 new product launches, marking its boldest innovation push in years

From next-gen hair bonding technology to powerful dry shampoos, upgraded men's care, and long-lasting deodorants, this launch reinforces Suave's commitment to giving consumers more for less. Available now at retailers nationwide, these new products combine cutting-edge science, premium ingredients, and trending scents—all priced between $1.99 and $5.99. With this launch, Suave cements its place as a leader in accessible beauty, proving that consumers don't need to spend big for top-tier results.

Salon Results, Drugstore Prices

"At Suave, we believe everyone deserves great beauty products—without overpaying," said Rachel Behm, Suave Brands Co. SVP of Marketing. "We're taking innovation to the next level, bringing high-performance formulas that compete with luxury brands at a fraction of the cost. With 34 new products launching now, we're making salon-level results available for under $5."

What's New?
(product fact sheets are linked below)

WOMEN'S HAIR CARE 

  • Bonding Infusion Collection: The Bonding Infusion Collection, which includes shampoo, conditioner, purple shampoo, and treatment mask, delivers advanced damage repair - marking the first time this technology is available for under $5. The shampoo and treatment mask combo repairs damage as well as Olaplex.*
  • Shampoo & Conditioners: Hyaluronic Infusion deeply moisturizes dry & dehydrated hair; Bamboo & Hibiscus gives hair an instant boost of weightless volume.
  • Dry Shampoos in 3 variants deliver instant refresh, volume, and invigoration, working as well as Kérastase+.
  • New Heat Protection Spray shields strands from heat damage against temperatures up to 450°F.

MEN'S CARE

  • Three new Antiperspirant Deodorants with 48-hour odor & sweat protection.
  • Stress Relief Collection: Incorporates the all-day fresh scent of lavender and cedarwood in a 2-in-1 Shampoo/Conditioner, Body + Face Wash, and Deodorant.
  • Barber Essentials Collection: Offers simple, easy-to-use styling products for all hair types, available in Styling Cream, Pomade, and Gel for pro-level grooming.

WOMEN'S BODY CARE

  • Reformulated Antiperspirant Deodorants with 20% more sweat-stopping power and up to 48 hours of protection. Suave also has aluminum-free options.
  • Strawberry Delight Body Wash features the uplifting and indulging notes of strawberry, caramel, and Chantilly cream, formulated with glycerin & Vitamin E for ultra-soft skin.

KIDS

  • Limited-Edition Minions Collection: Collectible designs in celebration of the wildly popular franchise. 3-in-1, Slime Body Wash, and Detangler offer kids fun new designs of their favorite bathtime products.

*Comparisons to Olaplex and Kerastase are based on independent testing. Suave Bonding Infusion Shampoo and Treatment Mask compared to Olaplex No. 4 Bond Maintenance Shampoo and No. 5 Bond Maintenance Conditioner. In-vivo salon study measuring hair appearance, damage repair, texture and strength.

+Refreshing Dry Shampoo by Suave compared to Kérastase Fresh Affair Dry Shampoo. In-vitro testing on hair tresses measuring volume & build-up removal.

About Suave Brands Company 
Suave Brands Company was established in 2023 after Yellow Wood Partners acquired the Suave brand from Unilever, transforming it into a standalone company. The Suave Brands Co. mission is to make trusted high-quality beauty and personal care products accessible to all.

For nearly 90 years, Suave has served consumers with exceptional products at a great value. Originally founded with a vision to democratize salon-quality care, the company has evolved to offer a comprehensive range of hair and body care products serving women, men and kids. Suave has been recognized as one of TIME's World's Best Brands and Newsweek's Most Trusted Brands. Suave products can be purchased at more than 60,000 retailers across the U.S., including Walmart, Dollar General, Target, CVS, Walgreens, Family Dollar, Kroger, and Amazon. For more information, please visit www.suave.com.

CONTACT:
Holly Thomas, holly.thomas@chameleon.co 

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/suave-brands-company-unleashes-its-biggest-product-drop-in-decades--34-new-innovations-across-hair-body--mens-care-302379120.html

SOURCE Suave Brands Co

data.title: Videogame giants are spending more money but losing players - and profits
data.description:
By Jurica Dujmovic

Sony, Electronic Arts and Ubisoft reflect an industrywide misunderstanding (or perhaps disregard) of what gamers value

Players are increasingly sensitive to and irritated by videogames that feel forced or inauthentic.

In recent years, the videogame industry has witnessed a significant shift in the relationship between major publishers and their core audience. Giants such as Ubisoft Entertainment (FR: UBI), Sony Group (SONY) (JP: 6758) (SONY), and Electronic Arts (EA) (EA) have become increasingly disconnected from the very players they claim to entertain. This divide stems from myriad factors, including changing gamer preferences, questionable business strategies and controversial creative decisions.

Repetitive gameplay and uninspired mechanics of tired formulas have left players fatigued and craving innovative and authentic experiences - the shift to which many major publishers are still struggling to adapt. One notable trend is the waning popularity of "live service" games - titles designed for endless play and continuous monetization. "Live service" games were once hailed as the future of gaming. However, players are increasingly turning away from games that demand constant time investment without offering meaningful progression or engagement.

On the other hand, games such as "Elden Ring" and "God of War: Ragnarok," which offer rich, single-player experiences that prioritize quality over endless looping gameplay are thriving.

As major publishers struggle, indie developers have seen a surge in popularity. Games such as "Hades," "Celeste" and "Hollow Knight" have resonated strongly with players, demonstrating that tightly focused, authentic experiences can often outshine big-budget releases.

Ubisoft: A study in disconnect

Perhaps no other company better exemplifies the challenges facing major publishers than Ubisoft. Once a darling of the industry, the French videogame publisher has found itself in increasingly troubled waters.

Ubisoft's financial woes are closely tied to a series of creative decisions that have alienated its core audience. One such example is "Star Wars Outlaws," which has significantly underperformed expectations. A report by Insider Gaming paints an even grimmer picture - according to the report, "Star Wars Outlaws" managed to sell just 1 million copies in the month after its launch late last August.

This disappointing performance is particularly concerning given the game's massive budget, with Ubisoft reportedly investing around $300 million into its development. Abysmal sales figures can be attributed to several factors, including a multitude of creative restrictions that resulted in a world that felt sanitized, censored and less engaging compared with other open-world experiences.

Another example is "XDefiant," Ubisoft's free-to-play shooter game. Despite a strong launch with more than 11 million unique players, Ubisoft announced late last year that it would shut down "XDefiant," highlighting the challenges for new entries in the saturated shooter-game market. The game faced criticism for lacking a strong identity and failing to offer groundbreaking features that could pull players away from established titles.

It's worth noting that not all of Ubisoft's recent ventures were failures. "Assassin's Creed Mirage," a return to the series' roots, managed to turn a profit, generating $250 million in revenue. This success suggests that there's still a strong market for well-crafted, focused experiences that respect the franchise's core identity.

Despite this glimmer of hope, Ubisoft's future strategy raises eyebrows among industry observers. The company plans to release 10 "Assassin's Creed" titles in the next five years, a decision that has been met with skepticism. Critics argue that this aggressive release schedule is unlikely to resolve the company's core issues and may exacerbate franchise fatigue and disconnect from their audience. Ubisoft's stock price took another hit recently after the company said that its much-anticipated "Assassin's Creed Shadows" offering was being delayed for a second time; it's now slated to debut in March.

Industry struggles

To survive and thrive, publishers will need to rebuild trust.

Ubisoft's struggles are not unique. Both EA and Sony have faced similar criticism and financial consequences when their games failed to meet players' expectations. EA's "Battlefield 2042" was lambasted for lacking core features and introducing unwanted hero-based systems, resulting in a 90% player drop within months and significant revenue losses. "Anthem," another EA title, failed due to shallow, unfinished content and rigid adherence to its live-service model.

Sony blowing $400 million on its failed "Concord" offering exemplifies the dangers of giving priority to stale mechanics, lackluster gameplay and political correctness over innovation and player engagement. Its development reportedly was marred by a "design by committee" approach that stifled creativity and forced progressive representation, leading to characters who lacked relatability, depth and originality. The focus on politics over player preferences, combined with lackluster and uninspired gameplay, resulted in "Concord" being pulled from sales shortly after release.

This industrywide trend points to a growing fundamental misunderstanding (or perhaps disregard) of what current-era gamers value. Players are increasingly sensitive to and irritated by narratives that feel forced or inauthentic. They also dislike worn and tired game mechanics and seek games that organically integrate story elements in meaningful and authentic ways, rather than check off inclusivity boxes. Gamers crave experiences that break the mold in new and inspiring ways, rather than tired cookie-cutter gameplay loops, devoid of fun and creativity.

To justify their missteps and uncreative decisions, major publishers often claim they're making games that speak to contemporary audience and reflect their sensibilities. Sales figures tell a different story. Their "modern demographics" appear to be little more than a vocal minority, whose influence in social-media discourse far outweighs their actual market impact. The discrepancy between perceived demand and actual market reception provides a clear explanation for the current state of the gaming industry, where traditional audiences continue to drive the bulk of revenue despite being increasingly alienated by attempts to appeal to a niche and more progressive player base.

The path forward for major publishers is clear: To survive and thrive, publishers will need to rebuild trust by embracing the values and identities that resonate with their player base. This means moving away from imposed narratives and forced political correctness in favor of authentic storytelling and gameplay that respect players' intelligence. Companies should focus on creating protagonists and stories that gamers can genuinely connect with.

To achieve this, publishers must engage in more open and honest communication with their communities through transparent development processes, clear explanations of creative decisions, and a genuine willingness to incorporate player feedback. Instead of trying to proselytize their audience, companies should listen to and learn from the gamers who have supported their franchises for years.

More: YouTube now dominates TV, streaming and even podcasts. Here's a look at how it got there.

Also read: Why GameStop might jump on board the bitcoin bandwagon

-Jurica Dujmovic

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-18-25 1204ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: My husband and I have been married for 18 years. We share a son - and my husband has a daughter. Why should they get an equal inheritance?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'This year she got married, and I was completely left out of any planning, bridal-shower and wedding activities'

Dear Quentin,

My husband and I have been married for 18 years and together for 19 years. We have a son together, and he has a daughter who is 10 years older than our son.

When we married, we combined all our financial accounts. My husband felt strongly that he should keep a separate life-insurance policy for his daughter that is valued at $100,000. I took out a life-insurance policy through work for a similar amount for our son. That will no longer be available after I retire next year.

Our wills split our money, with a third going to charity, a third to his daughter and a third to our son. While I have never been a huge fan of this distribution, over the past year I have become unhappy for two reasons.

First, his daughter will receive a separate inheritance from her mother.

The second, larger issue is emotional. I have treated her like a bonus daughter. She is in every family picture (the same is not true of her mother and her mother's second family).

I attended every school event and every sporting event, contributed equally to her college fund, included her on every family vacation - even though she is 28, she contributes almost nothing to those trips, and her dad does not wish to force her to as she would otherwise not likely attend, even though we are just talking about a cabin and nothing elaborate. Birthdays and holidays are treated equally for each of the kids.

I wanted to be sure to wear an understated outfit for the mother of the bride's sake, and yet be dressed nicely as a person who has been in her life since she was 8 years old.

This year she got married, and I was completely left out of any planning, bridal-shower and wedding activities. Her mother and I get along without drama. In fact, her mother said she wanted me to be included because I was part of her family. When I asked my stepdaughter to go shopping together for my dress, her response was "get whatever you want."

I wanted to be sure to wear an understated outfit for the mother of the bride's sake, and yet be dressed nicely as a person who has been in her life since she was 8 years old. It is obvious to me that my husband's former wife views me as a person who is her husband's wife, and not as a friend. That makes me sad as I viewed her differently and we have never had cross words.

Since my stepdaughter is my son's only sibling, I do want that relationship maintained. However, I am becoming uncomfortable with her receiving an equal inheritance. I came from almost nothing and have worked extremely hard to be financially secure. She has a job but expects money to be handed to her. My son, on the other hand, is a go-getter and is working very hard.

When I am gone, I am gone. But I am having a hard time leaving her half of my hard-earned savings. Thoughts?

The Stepmother

Related: My stepdaughter is executor to her late father's will, and believes she's now on the deed to my home. Is that possible?

Dear Stepmother,

You can't be liked by everyone, as much as you try.

You could fly to the moon and back and still not get a "welcome home" from your stepdaughter, or a card congratulating you on your trip from your husband's former wife. The more you do for your stepdaughter, the more you believe she should appreciate you, respect you and like you. But life doesn't work that way. In fact, your efforts may be having the opposite effect. She might even consider you pushy, even if that is unjust.

Yes, it would have been nice for her to acknowledge your position in the family during her wedding celebrations, given everything you have done for her over the years, even if she felt agnostic about your presence at her wedding or in her life. But this is a time for her to do things the way she wants them done. Your frustration with her is likely mirrored by her eye rolls. You may have to accept that she's not going to come around.

You and your husband have two financial hurdles to overcome: the decision about how to split any inheritance, and how you make that decision.

You and your husband have two financial hurdles to overcome: the decision about how to split any inheritance, and how you make that decision. You could go to battle with him and argue that you would like the satisfaction of knowing that your son will receive as close as possible to 100% of your separate assets. This may be a difficult task given that you have commingled so many of your marital assets, including - I presume - your home.

There are ways you can divert funds to your son. First, if you own your house through tenancy in common, you can deed your 50% share to your son. Second, you and your husband can divide your joint accounts 50/50, and you can add your son as a beneficiary to your own. It may not get him to 100%, but it could get him pretty close. Third, you can set up a trust that becomes irrevocable upon either one of your deaths so that neither of you can alter the terms on their own.

Even if you and your husband come to a mutually agreeable arrangement, taking such precautions would give you peace of mind that your husband won't have a change of heart if you should die first. If you simply write a will together, your wishes would not necessarily be carried out if he outlived you. He could, for example, decide to split your marital estate 50/50 between your son and his daughter. After all, it might make more sense for him to want to leave each of his kids an equal share.

On the upside, if you did go first, you would never need to know.

Related: 'Don't be naïve': I have a wake-up call for divorcing women - you've been giving up too much for too long. Am I wrong?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com, and follow Quentin Fottrell on X, the platform formerly known as Twitter.

The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

'I'm struggling with grief and loss': I inherited seven figures after my parents died young. Why do I feel guilty?

I'm 54 and have terminal cancer. I have a wife, 47, and 8-year-old child. How do I split my $1.2 million retirement and life insurance between them?

'She's the queen of CDs': My mother-in-law, 83, opened 12 CDs at different financial institutions. Should I intervene?

Check out The Moneyist's private Facebook group, where members help answer life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions, or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By emailing your questions to The Moneyist or posting your dilemmas on The Moneyist Facebook group, you agree to have them published anonymously on MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

-Quentin Fottrell

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-18-25 1148ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Home builders just got a reality check - and it's not good news for home buyers
data.description:
By Aarthi Swaminathan

'Policy uncertainty and cost factors created a reset for 2025 expectations,' industry group says

The numbers: Home-builder confidence plunged to the lowest level in five months as concerns over tariffs and how they could raise the cost of housing weighed on the industry.

Sentiment fell in February, mainly because of a sharp drop in builders' expectations for sales of newly built homes over the next six months.

The National Association of Home Builders' monthly confidence index fell five points to 42 in February, the industry group said on Tuesday.

The February figure fell short of expectations on Wall Street. A year ago, the index stood at 48.

The decline in confidence is a significant change in sentiment among home builders.

The shift comes after President Donald Trump first announced and then scaled back tariffs. Builders had initially asked for exemptions from the tariffs, but this latest report indicates that they have readjusted their expectations in recent weeks.

"While builders hold out hope for pro-development policies, particularly for regulatory reform, policy uncertainty and cost factors created a reset for 2025 expectations," Carl Harris, a custom home builder from Wichita, Kan., and the chair of the NAHB, said in a statement.

For buyers, the sentiment shift only adds to the likelihood that home prices could go up in an already expensive housing market.

Key details: Trump's tariffs on imported goods from Canada, China and Mexico are increasing the cost of building materials, which is spooking home builders.

Even though the president has paused some of those tariffs, uncertainty over whether they will be reinstated is affecting how builders see the road ahead for the housing market.

Buyers will likely be affected by the tariffs as well, because as the cost of construction rises, so does the sale price of a new home. Companies typically pass tariffs - which constitute an additional cost - along to consumers, according to the Economic Policy Institute, a left-leaning think tank.

The average cost of a newly constructed home in the U.S. is currently about $422,000, according to one estimate from CoreLogic, a real-estate data company. Higher costs for building materials due to tariffs could add between $17,000 and $22,000 to that price tag, the company estimated.

"With 32% of appliances and 30% of softwood lumber coming from international trade, uncertainty over the scale and scope of tariffs has builders further concerned about costs," said Robert Dietz, chief economist at the NAHB.

Mortgage rates also remain high, another challenge for home builders as demand remains weaker than normal in the face of high housing costs. The 30-year mortgage rate is still higher than it was before the pandemic, averaging 6.87% as of Feb. 13, according to Freddie Mac.

Even before the Trump tariffs were announced, the market was not affordable, Matt Saunders, a senior vice president at John Burns Research and Consulting, told MarketWatch. "This is just adding to an existing issue," he said. Tariffs are "definitely an affordability shock."

Builders have pulled back on incentives, even as they grapple with high inventory levels.

Only 26% of builders were cutting home prices, the lowest share since May 2024. A month ago, 30% of builders were cutting prices.

The average price cut was 5% in February, unchanged from the previous month.

About 59% of builders were using sales incentives other than price cuts to improve sales in February, down from 61% in the previous month.

Those incentives include offering mortgage-rate buydowns, where the builder offers the buyer a lower interest rate for a set period before it adjusts upwards.

The three gauges that underpin the overall builder-confidence index were down:

-- Builders were downbeat about current sales conditions. That gauge fell 4 points.

-- Builders expect a drop in traffic from prospective buyers. That gauge fell by 3 points.

-- Builders were more pessimistic about future sales. That gauge fell 13 points.

Big picture: Home builders have been on a bumpy ride under the Trump administration - and it's only been a month.

Builders initially expected a second Trump administration to offer fewer regulatory roadblocks around the construction of new homes.

When the tariffs were announced, builders asked the White House for an exemption, because the housing sector was already in crisis due to a lack of affordable homes, with many buyers unable to purchase homes at current prices and interest rates.

Builders have since changed their expectations, as seen in the latest report from the NAHB. "The more eye-catching number in this report ... is the 13-point plunge in the index of expected sales over the next six months," Oliver Allen, senior U.S. economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics, wrote in a note. The title of his note was "Reality bites for homebuilders."

"Until this month, homebuilders seem to have unrealistically shrugged off the very high level of mortgage rates; the latest slump in the expectations index takes it back to the level implied by still depressed mortgage demand," Allen said.

Read more: 6 ways a second Trump presidency will affect home buyers and sellers

The next few months could continue to test home builders. On top of tariffs, they are also likely to face more competition from the resale market during the typically hot spring home-buying season.

More homeowners are slashing prices on their homes to attract offers, and with more sellers dipping into the market, builders could face more pressure to offer attractive deals on mortgage rates or to reduce home prices.

Nearly one in five homeowners who are listing their homes have cut their asking price, according to a report by real-estate platform Zillow. New listings of existing homes were also up 7.4% as of Feb. 9 compared with a year ago, according to real-estate brokerage Redfin.

-Aarthi Swaminathan

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-18-25 1151ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Nozomi Networks Named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms
data.description:
Nozomi Networks Named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms

Nozomi Networks Named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms

PR Newswire

SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 18, 2025

Company Recognized for Ability to Execute, and Completeness of Vision

SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Nozomi Networks, the leader in OT, IoT and CPS security, today announced it has been named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms.

According to Gartner, cyber-physical systems (CPS) protection platforms vendors offer products and services that use knowledge of industrial protocols, operational/production network packets or traffic metadata, and physical process asset behavior to discover, categorize, map and protect cyber-physical systems in production or mission-critical environments outside of enterprise IT. Gartner also noted that, "cyber-physical systems protection platforms that discover and protect assets in production or mission-critical environments are emerging as a leading market category."

"We are honored that Gartner has recognized Nozomi Networks as a Leader in its first Magic Quadrant for CPS protection platforms," said CEO Edgard Capdevielle. "We believe this recognition demonstrates Nozomi's capabilities in leveraging AI for asset discovery as well as threat and vulnerability management, and it underscores our longstanding commitment to innovation and partnership with our customers. We are dedicated to empowering organizations with better ways to secure critical infrastructure, mitigating risk and maximizing resilience from the endpoint to the air."

Read the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms here.

Since 2013, Nozomi Networks has been a pioneer in OT, IoT and cyber-physical systems security. Our people, partners and fully integrated, AI-powered CPS protection platform deliver unmatched visibility, proactive threat detection, and real-time insights.

With the Nozomi Networks Platform, CISOs and their security teams benefit from:

  • Unparalleled asset discovery and inventory across wired, wireless, endpoint and ICS endpoint attack surfaces
  • Sophisticated, actionable vulnerability management
  • Fast and accurate anomaly and threat detection and management
  • A comprehensive view of CPS cyber risk and risk mitigation strategies from the asset to the enterprise
  • AI-powered prioritizations and remediation recommendations

The platform excels at delivering operational resilience, risk management and regulatory compliance outcomes to customers across a wide array of OT and IoT verticals and use cases.

Nozomi Networks helps customers in six continents around the world manage risk and maximize resilience at every stage of their digital journey. In addition to being recognized as a Leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant, Nozomi Networks is rated 4.9 based on 126 reviews as of 14 February 2025 in Gartner Peer Insight Reviews for OT Security. 

According to the OT Cybersecurity Manager of IT Security and Risk Management for an energy and utility company, "The solutions provided by Nozomi proved to be the best choice possible to protect and monitor our industrial sites. Through its installation in our plants, we are obtaining a complete overview of the industrial assets and are able to find out in real time threats, vulnerabilities and any kind of noncompliance – being able to control that any control and maintenance activity is properly traced, be that performed locally or remotely."

And the Cyber Security Engineer of IT Security and Risk Management for a real estate company said, "Nozomi is a powerful and flexible tool that offers significant value to organizations looking to enhance their security posture. They have excellent support, high customizability, and strong incident management capabilities. It is without a doubt well-suited for any organization with complex and evolving security needs."

To learn more about Nozomi Networks and the Nozomi Networks Platform:

  • Read our Blog: Nozomi Networks Is a Leader in 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms 
  • Sign up for a live demo

Gartner, Magic Quadrant for CPS Protection Platforms, Katell Thielemann, Wam Voster, Ruggero Contu, 12 February 2025

data.title: ICE Canola: Oilseed Holding Firm
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures were narrowly mixed late Tuesday morning, getting some support from comparable oils.

Increases in the Chicago soy complex and European rapeseed spilled over into the canola, while Malaysian palm oil was relatively steady. Upticks in crude oil added to the gains in the vegetable oils.

The May canola contract remained well above its major moving averages which generated more support.

The tightening canola supply situation continued to underpin the oilseed's values, but ongoing tariff threats from the United States fueled caution in the markets.

The Canadian dollar was firm at mid-session Tuesday, with the loonie at 70.48 U.S. cents compared to Friday close of 70.43.

Approximately 29,550 canola contracts were traded as of 11:14 am EST, with prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne: 

 
           Price      Change 
Mar       663.30     dn 0.20 
May       674.70     up 0.20 
Jul       680.00     up 0.60 
Nov       655.70     up 2.00

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 18, 2025 11:42 ET (16:42 GMT)

data.title: Jollibee Partners with Botrista to Launch Signature Sips Nationwide
data.description:
Jollibee Partners with Botrista to Launch Signature Sips Nationwide

Jollibee Partners with Botrista to Launch Signature Sips Nationwide

PR Newswire

SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 18, 2025

SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- In a strategic move that signals the growing importance of automated beverage solutions in the quick-service restaurant industry, Jollibee Foods Corporation (JFC), one of Asia's largest restaurant companies, has partnered with Botrista to revolutionize their beverage program across their U.S. locations.

"Botrista is a game changer for the beverage industry. We are excited to invest in a company that creates excellent and innovative products, enabling food service operators to deliver a world-class customer experience and providing a substantial runway for sustained profitable growth," JFC chairman Tony Tan Caktiong said.

This landmark partnership brings together Jollibee's global restaurant expertise with Botrista's full beverage program, leveraging their automation technology and Michelin-method drink mastery - creating the perfect partnership to launch their new "Signature Sips" menu.

"Jollibee has an extremely high bar when it comes to ingredient standards and top-quality products, making a partnership with them a perfect blend of product thoughtfulness," said Sean Hsu, CEO at Botrista. "Together, we're creating an elevated beverage experience that complements their iconic food offerings while maintaining consistent quality at scale."

The partnership comes at a time when consumer demand for creative, customizable beverages continues to surge. Industry research by Botrista shows that 84% of consumers seek unique experiences and bonding when dining out - a key factor in Jollibee's partnership with the company.

"We approached the development of the Signature Sips menu with the goal of offering a premium drink experience that would both complement our best-selling food items and encourage people to try Jollibee for the first time," said Nick Bedell, marketing director of Jollibee North America. "Our team drew inspiration from tropical flavors as we tested 46 flavor variations and hundreds of recipes over a rapid process of just 78 days. The result is the unique and refreshing selection we have today."

The new Signature Sips menu, powered by Botrista's technology, features an extensive lineup including:

  • Fruit-forward Quenchers like the Tropical Breeze (strawberry, passionfruit, and coconut blend) and Dragonfruit Sunset
  • Creamy frozen Freezes including Coconut Dream and Strawberry Bliss
  • Premium Teas such as Passionfruit Iced Tea and Lychee Iced Tea
  • Specialty items like Sweet Cream Cold Brew

Botrista's plug-and-play platform enables Jollibee restaurants to:

  • Introduce premium craft beverages with minimal additional training
  • Create region-specific drink offerings catering to local tastes
  • Maintain consistent quality across locations while reducing preparation time
  • Generate new revenue streams through an expanded beverage menu

"We're thrilled to bring these new refreshing drinks to our customers and offer them an elevated and delightful beverage experience that complements our menu perfectly," says Luis Velasco, senior vice president and marketing head at Jollibee Foods Corporation, North America.

"Whether it's a midday pick-me-up or a treat to enjoy with your meal, we want every sip to bring a little extra joy to your day. Botrista is making that happen for us."

The initial rollout has already shown promising results, with participating restaurants reporting significant increases in beverage sales and customer satisfaction scores. As the partnership develops, both companies plan to collaborate on developing new beverage innovations specifically tailored for various markets, where premium drinks and milk teas continue to see strong growth.

About Jollibee Foods Corporation 

Jollibee Foods Corporation (JFC) is one of Asia's largest food service companies operating in multiple markets worldwide. With more than 70 locations in the United States and over 1,650 across 17 countries, Jollibee is one of the world's fastest-growing restaurant chains. The company operates diverse food concepts, including Jollibee, Chowking, Greenwich, Red Ribbon, Mang Inasal, and has expanded its presence internationally through strategic investments and partnerships.

About Botrista 

Botrista enables partners to easily serve an array of vibrant drinks in order to tackle dynamic consumer trends. With incredible ingredients sourced from around the globe - consumers looking for trending, cold beverages are spoiled for choice. By utilizing an extremely detailed data-driven approach to menu development. Implemented, operated and scaled without any added complexity - Botrista's ever-evolving partnership delivers on quality, consistency and profit. It's 'out of the box' thinking, just in a cup.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/jollibee-partners-with-botrista-to-launch-signature-sips-nationwide-302379116.html

SOURCE Botrista Inc.

data.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Currently Down Eight Consecutive Days, on Track for Longest Losing Streak Since September 2022 — Data Talk
data.description:
Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. ( TMO ) is currently at $526.65, down $5.20 or 0.98% 




     --Would be lowest close since Jan. 2, 2025, when it closed at $522.57 



     --Currently down 11 of the past 12 days 



     --Currently down eight consecutive days; down 9.57% over this period 



     --Longest losing streak since Sept. 22, 2022, when it fell for eight straight trading days 



     --Worst eight day stretch since the eight days ending Nov. 18, 2024, when it fell 10.43% 



     --Down 11.89% month-to-date 



     --Up 1.23% year-to-date 



     --Down 21.07% from its all-time closing high of $667.24 on Dec. 31, 2021 



     --Down 3.92% from 52 weeks ago (Feb. 20, 2024), when it closed at $548.14 



     --Down 15.63% from its 52-week closing high of $624.21 on Sept. 10, 2024
